title: crocanthemum rosmarinifolium
intro: Crocanthemum rosmarinifolium, also known as Rosmarinifolia Hill's Heath, is a shrubby perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is native to the southeastern United States and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Crocanthemum rosmarinifolium can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to soothe sore throats, coughs, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
